active_ingredients: Fexofenadine hydrochloride 30 mg, 60 mg, 180 mg tablets; 6 mg/mL oral suspension (e.g., Allegra)
indications_and_usage: Uses: temporarily relieves the symptoms of hay fever and other upper respiratory allergies - runny nose, sneezing, itchy/watery eyes, itching of the nose or throat; also relieves itching due to hives. It is a non-drowsy second-generation antihistamine and is one of the least sedating.
contraindications: Do not use if you have ever had an allergic reaction to fexofenadine.
warnings_and_cautions: Ask a doctor before use if you have kidney disease (a lower dose may be needed). Do not take with fruit juices (apple, orange, grapefruit) - they can reduce how much fexofenadine is absorbed; take with water. Stop use and ask a doctor if an allergic reaction occurs or hives last more than 6 weeks. Causes drowsiness in very few people, but if it does, do not drive or operate machinery.
adverse_reactions: Headache, drowsiness (uncommon), dizziness, nausea; in children - cough, upper respiratory infection symptoms.
drug_interactions: Antacids containing aluminum and magnesium reduce absorption - separate by 2 hours. Fruit juices reduce absorption - take with water. Erythromycin and ketoconazole raise fexofenadine levels modestly (usually not significant). P-glycoprotein inhibitors/inducers can alter levels.
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: 60 mg twice daily, or 180 mg once daily, with water; do not exceed 180 mg in 24 hours. Children 2 to under 12 years: 30 mg twice daily (suspension or 30 mg tablet). Children 6 months to under 2 years (for hives): 15 mg twice daily. Children under 6 months: ask a doctor. Kidney disease: reduce frequency or ask a doctor.
overdosage: Overdose has caused dizziness, drowsiness, and dry mouth; generally not dangerous. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
